gazette: General Directorate for International Agreements and European Union
title: Further clarifications made by the Trade Ministry regarding TR&UK FTA
bodyHtml:
The General Directorate of International Agreements and European Union and the General Directorate of Customs published notices containing clarifications regarding the origin declarations issued within the scope of the Turkey-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement.

For exporters located in Turkey, when the origin declaration is made out by an approved exporter, the authorization number of the approved exporter must be entered in this space. When the origin declaration is not made out by an approved exporter, the words in brackets shall be omitted or the space left blank.

On the other hand, the EORI number must be entered in this space when the origin declaration is made out by an exporter located in the UK.

When exporting to Turkey the UK exporters must include their EORI number in any declaration they issue to their Turkish customer, regardless of the value.

Regarding the origin of the statement issued by the UK exporter;

-Preferential tariff requests for declarations of origin that do not contain an EORI number and any kind of signature (wet signature, signature image, electronic signature) should not be accepted, 

-With regard to declarations of origin without an EORI number but a wet signature; preferential tariff requests shall not be accepted and the Turkish importer will be given 30 days to submit a valid declaration of origin with an EORI number.  

-In cases where the EORI number is entered in the relevant part of the text of the declaration of origin, the declarations of origin do not have to be signed.

- the declaration of origin in which the EORI number is indicated anywhere on the invoice other than the relevant field in the text of the declaration of origin, shall not be accepted. A request to conduct a verification of origin shall be made by the Turkish customs to the UK Customs Administration (specifying the reason for the inquiry) and action should be taken according to the response to be received.
